operating_state: Revenue declined 17% year-over-year to $5.93 billion as Gaming contracted 51% from partner inventory reduction amid macroeconomic headwinds and China COVID lockdowns. Compute & Networking grew 31% with cloud provider strength and initial H100 Hopper shipments, while Professional Visualization fell 65% on channel inventory realignment and Automotive surged 86% on self-driving demand. New U.S. export restrictions on China sales required launch of alternative A800 products within regulatory constraints.
cost_structure: Gross margin compressed 11.6 percentage points to 53.6% primarily from $702 million inventory charge on excess demand projections. Operating expenses increased 31% driven by compensation costs, employee growth, and data center infrastructure investments. Additional pressure from $122 million warranty reserves for Data Center component defects and premium payments for extended supply lead times.
material_events: Terminated Arm acquisition recording $1.35 billion termination cost from prepayment write-off. U.S. government imposed export license requirements for A100, H100, and high-performance systems to China and Russia during Q3. Closed Russian operations following sales suspension. Returned $3.75 billion to shareholders with $8.28 billion remaining under authorization through December 2023.
macro_exposures: Export restrictions create competitive disadvantage targeting NVIDIA's AI-capable products with licensing uncertainty affecting China customer relationships. Geopolitical tensions involving Taiwan and China threaten supply chain continuity given manufacturing concentration. COVID lockdowns continue constraining supply chains and reducing end customer demand. Graphics card tariff exclusions expire December 2022 without guaranteed extension.
forward_outlook: Management expects fiscal 2023 capital investments of $1.80-2.00 billion. Continued China revenue impact expected from export restrictions with uncertain license approval timing. Company transitioning certain operations out of China creating potential costly disruptions. Architectural transitions for Gaming and Data Center products may continue affecting demand predictability.
uncertainty_profile: Export license timing and scope remain uncertain with potential for additional multilateral AI semiconductor controls. Inventory provisions totaling $2.01 billion reflect demand forecasting challenges amid volatile cryptocurrency markets and Ethereum transition. Chinese gaming restrictions may affect revenues. Unrecognized tax benefits of $869 million under IRS examination with uncertain timing.
